title: RIO Receipt Protocol Specification
description: Defines the data structures that form the complete chain of custody for a governed AI action under the RIO protocol. v2.1 adds ingestion provenance tracking and Ed25519 identity binding to receipts.
author: Brian Kent Rasmussen

changes: Added 'ingestion' object to ActionRequest for source provenance tracking
Added 'identity_binding' object to Receipt for Ed25519 signer proof
Added 'kill_switch' receipt type to Receipt status enum
Added 'onboard' receipt type for welcome receipts
Preserved full backward compatibility with v2.0.0

ActionRequest.description: An immutable record of a proposed action submitted by an AI agent or upstream system. Once created, the request cannot be modified.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this action request.

action_type.description: The type of action being requested.

requested_by.description: Identifier of the AI agent or system that submitted this request.

target.description: The target system or resource the action will affect.

parameters.description: Action-specific parameters. Structure varies by action_type.

risk_level.description: Assessed risk level of the proposed action.

context.description: Optional free-text context explaining why this action is being requested.

ingestion.description: Provenance metadata recording how this intent entered the system. Added in v2.1.

source.description: The channel through which this intent was received.

source_message_id.description: Original message ID from the source system (e.g., Outlook message ID, Twilio SID).

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the intent was received by the gateway.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this request was created.

AIRecommendation.description: The AI system's evaluation of an ActionRequest against the active policy context.

request_id.description: The request_id of the ActionRequest being evaluated.

recommended_action.description: The AI's recommendation for how to handle this request.

confidence.description: Confidence score for the recommendation (0.0 = no confidence, 1.0 = full confidence).

reasoning.description: Explanation of the factors considered in making this recommendation.

unknowns.description: Explicit declaration of assumptions or unknowns that affect confidence.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this recommendation was generated.

ApprovalRecord.description: A cryptographically signed, time-limited authorization from a human authority. Each approval is bound to a specific request_id and can only be used once.

approval_id.description: Unique identifier for this approval record.

request_id.description: The request_id of the ActionRequest being authorized.

approver_id.description: Identifier of the human who issued this approval. Derived from authenticated session, not client-supplied.

approver_role.description: The organizational role of the approver at the time of approval.

signer_id.description: The registered signer ID used for Ed25519 signature. Added in v2.1.

approval_status.description: The outcome of the human authorization decision.

approved_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the human issued this approval.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this approval expires. Default TTL: 300 seconds.

auth_method.description: The authentication method used to verify the approver's identity.

conditions.description: Optional conditions or constraints attached to the approval.

signature.description: Cryptographic signature over the canonical approval payload.

signature_algorithm.description: The algorithm used to generate the signature.

ExecutionRecord.description: The record of what was actually executed against the target system.

execution_id.description: Unique identifier for this execution record.

request_id.description: The request_id of the original ActionRequest.

approval_id.description: The approval_id of the ApprovalRecord that authorized this execution.

executed_by.description: Identifier of the system or service that performed the execution.

executed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the execution occurred.

execution_status.description: The outcome of the execution attempt.

execution_token_id.description: One-time token consumed during execution, proving authorization and preventing replay.

result.description: The result returned by the target system.

connector.description: The connector used to dispatch the action.

Receipt.description: The final, immutable record binding all preceding artifacts into a single signed document. Receipts form a hash-chained ledger. v2.1 adds identity_binding for Ed25519 signer proof.

receipt_type.description: The type of receipt. Added in v2.1. 'governed_action' is the standard pipeline receipt. 'kill_switch' records emergency proxy shutdown. 'onboard' records new user welcome. 'system' records system-level events.

request_id.description: The request_id of the original ActionRequest.

action_type.description: The action type from the original request.

requested_by.description: The agent that requested the action.

approver_id.description: The human who approved the action.

executed_by.description: The system that executed the action.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this receipt was generated.

ingestion.description: Provenance metadata from the original ActionRequest. Added in v2.1.

identity_binding.description: Ed25519 identity binding proof linking the approval to a registered signer. Added in v2.1.

signer_id.description: The registered signer ID from the authorized_signers table.

public_key_hex.description: The Ed25519 public key (64-char hex) of the signer at the time of approval.

signature_payload_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical JSON payload that was signed.

governance_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the governance decision.

receipt_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of this receipt (covers all preceding hashes).

ledger_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical receipt content, used for ledger chain integrity.

previous_hash.description: The ledger_hash of the immediately preceding receipt. Value is 'GENESIS' for the first receipt in the chain.

signature.description: Cryptographic signature over the canonical receipt content.

status.description: The verification status of this receipt.

hash_specification.serialization: Deterministic JSON (keys sorted alphabetically, no whitespace, UTF-8 encoding)

ingestion_sources.description: Canonical list of ingestion source identifiers. Each intent entering the system must declare its source.

sources.description: Direct API call via POST /intent or POST /api/v1/intents
Email-originated intent via Outlook → Power Automate → Service Bus
Intent submitted from the Jordan dashboard UI
